calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-22-2016 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-18-2016 Continued To Jan-11-17 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Motion To Seal Briefs And Evidence In Support Of Motion To Modify Injunction To Require Arbitration Of Second Carve-Out To Separation Agreement
Off Calendar Jul-07-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT KENNETH MINN'S Motion To Seal Briefs And Evidence In Support Of Motion To Modify Injunction To Require Arbitration Of Second Carve-Out To Separation Agreement is granted. No opposition filed and the requirements of sealing rules have been satisfied. Order signed. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/hk

(Redacted) Motion To Modify Injunction To Require Arbitration Of Second Carve-Out To Separation Agreement
Off Calendar Jun-23-2016 Off Calendar

Order To Show Cause Regarding Preliminary Injunction To Enjoin Arbitration
Off Calendar May-11-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Virginia DeMarchi of Fenwick & West LLP telephone number 650-988-8500 for Plaintiff Daymen US, Inc.
Marcus Hall of Troiutman Sanders telephone number 415-477-5716 for Defendant Kenu, Inc. and David Yao.

The Court confirmed its tentative ruling. Order To Show Cause Regarding Preliminary Injunction To Enjoin Arbitration is granted in part. Except for the issue of the enforceability and scope of the so-called first "carve-out" in paragraph 5 of the February 11, 2011 separation agreement between Mr. Minn and Joby, Inc., including whether that carve-out permitted Mr. Minn to engage in the work that led to the asserted inventions documented in and protected by the 707 and 714 patents, Mr. Minn is preliminarily enjoined from proceeding with the arbitration. The issue regarding the first carve-out is a logical and legal predicate to the determination of one or more of the claims asserted by DayMen in case number 547819. DayMen has not advanced a persuasive argument why that issue should not be resolved first or why it isn't covered by the arbitration clause agreed to by its predecessor Joby. However, Mr. Minn has not advanced a persuasive argument why any other issues should be arbitrated in light of the lawsuit filed by DayMen in case number 547819. If and when an arbitration award on the carve-out issue is confirmed, Mr. Minn may file a motion to dissolve the injunction if he has good cause to do so. Order signed in open Court.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)
